title: 5.5 The Immune System
description: The immune system — every fact from the Grade 10 Biology textbook.

questions.question: How is immunity defined?
options: The body's ability to produce hormones
The body's ability to prevent the invasion of pathogens
The body's ability to digest food
The body's ability to circulate blood
questions.correctAnswer: The body's ability to prevent the invasion of pathogens

questions.question: Against what types of organisms does the immune system defend?
options: Viruses, bacteria, fungal infections, and parasites
questions.correctAnswer: Viruses, bacteria, fungal infections, and parasites

questions.question: The lymphatic system is part of which broader system?
What are the two types of immune systems?
What is the body's initial line of defense against invading microbial diseases?
What do Pattern recognition receptors (PRRs) produced by innate immune cells help to maintain?
What type of lines of defense does innate immunity have?
What chemical agents are produced to attract immune cells to infection locations?
What cascade is activated to recognize bacteria, stimulate cells, and facilitate clearance of dead cells?
What specialized cells identify and remove foreign compounds found in organs, tissues, blood, and lymph?
When is adaptive (acquired) immunity formed?
options: After an initial reaction to a new pathogen
questions.correctAnswer: After an initial reaction to a new pathogen

questions.question: What is vaccination based on?
During antigen presentation, what type of antigens are recognized by the adaptive immune system?
What is the development of targeted responses to eradicate specific infections called?
What is the process through which signature antibodies or T cell receptors remember each infection called?
What cells can be summoned to eliminate an invader swiftly if another infection arises?
Which white blood cells travel between the bloodstream and the lymphatic system?
What are the three most common types of lymphocytes?
options: T cells, B cells, and natural killer cells
Helper T cells, cytotoxic T cells, suppressor T cells
questions.correctAnswer: T cells, B cells, and natural killer cells

questions.question: Where are lymphocytes formed?
Where do some lymphocytes move to mature into T cells?
Where do some lymphocytes mature into B cells?
What is the most common form of white blood cell?
What vital role do neutrophils play?
What type of cell is a phagocyte?
options: A cell that engulfs and digests cellular debris and pathogens
questions.correctAnswer: A cell that engulfs and digests cellular debris and pathogens

questions.question: What chemical signal causes neutrophils to be promptly recruited to the site of injury or infection?
Where are neutrophils ordinarily located?
Macrophages play a role in which immune systems?
What are targets of macrophages?
options: Foreign chemicals, pathogenic bacteria, and cancer cells
questions.correctAnswer: Foreign chemicals, pathogenic bacteria, and cancer cells

questions.question: What cells do macrophages stimulate to respond to pathogens?
What type of cells serve as intermediaries between the innate and adaptive immune systems?
Where are dendritic cells frequently found?
options: In tissues that contact the outside world
questions.correctAnswer: In tissues that contact the outside world

questions.question: Where do dendritic cells travel in response to infections?
What cells do dendritic cells interact with to start the adaptive immune response?
What is another name for antibodies?
What shape are antibodies?
Which cells produce antibodies?
What do antibodies bind to on the surface of foreign substances?
What do antibodies recognize and identify the foreign thing as?
What are the hormones produced by the immune system commonly called?
What effect do steroids and corticosteroids have on the immune system?
